raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/year>, my credit card account ending in XXXX was charged {$1400.00} by XXXX XXXX XXXX  ( XXXX, FL location ) under Agreement # XXXX. 

I disputed this charge with Barclays because I had purchased XXXX XXXX XXXX ( XXXX ) coverage for {$170.00} at the time of rental, which the company presented as full coverage. The rental was under my fathers name ( he is an authorized user ), but I am the primary cardholder. 

Heres what happened : - My fathers personal auto insurance was denied at the counter. 

- I tried to add myself as the driver so I could use my XXXX insurance, but that was also denied. 

- Because of this, I was forced to buy their XXXX, which they said would fully protect me. 

- Later, the car was hit in a no-fault parking lot accident, which we properly reported. 

- Even though I had XXXX, XXXX  charged my account {$1400.00} for damage, loss of use, and fees. 

- To make matters worse, XXXX  has never shown me proof that they didnt also go after the other drivers insurance, which makes me worry they may be collecting twice. 

I gave Barclays all of this documentation, but they still denied my dispute and sided with the merchant. After that, I tried to appeal the decision, but Barclays never got back to me. I even called and was told Id get a call back, but nobody followed up. 

This feels incredibly unfair. I bought coverage in good faith, and I expected my bank to protect me when the merchant misrepresented it. Instead, Ive been left paying {$1400.00} for something that should have been covered. 


Attachments : - Rental agreement showing XXXX purchase - Demand letter from XXXX - Claims email showing the case was closed - XXXX  correspondence
